w:r.w:t: The requirement for the Degree of

for his valuable and continuous guidance for the preparation of the Internship Training report.

I, the undersigned student of TYBCA (Sem. VI) (A.Y. 2025-26), here by declare that Internship Report prepared by me at below mentioned company, is the original work carried out by me and is the result of my hard work and compliance of all the policies and guidelines framed by the University, College and the Company concerned. I also declare that this report has been originally created by me and has not been published anywhere else other than submitted to SDJ International College,
Mission: To provide robust web solutions to customers across the globe. Vision: To utilize technology with excellence of skills and create a smooth user experience.

Website Design and Development, Mobile Application Development, E-Commerce Website Development, Software Development, UI/UX Design, Digital Marketing, SEO, Website Support and Maintenance, IT Consultation and Training Services.
Development Department, Digital Marketing Department, Designing Department, HR Department, Project Management Department
Web Development Department: Handles client-side design, backend development, maintenance, and testing of web applications.
Project Management Department: Coordinates requirements, timelines, and delivery quality.
Quality Assurance Support: Assists in reviewing functionality, defects, and usability before release.
Supervisor's Role in the Project: Mentor and technical guide
To apply theoretical knowledge of web development in a real software project.
To understand how frontend, backend, and database components work together in a production-style application.
To learn documentation and reporting practices followed during professional internship work.
To improve React.js, Node.js, Express.js, and PostgreSQL development skills.
To strengthen API integration, validation, debugging, and responsive UI implementation.
To gain practical experience in authentication flow and role-based module development.
To improve time management and consistency in daily development work.
To develop confidence in solving technical issues independently.
To adapt to teamwork and professional communication in an IT company environment.

This internship was completed at LYNX SOFTECH in the Web Development Department. During the internship, I contributed to the Quick Staff Hiring Portal, a full-stack web application designed to connect clients with on-demand workers and service professionals.

My major contribution focused on the Home, Authorization, and Worker modules. I developed responsive user interfaces, integrated frontend pages with backend APIs, worked with role-based access flow, and helped implement worker-side job and profile management features using React.js, Node.js, Express.js, and PostgreSQL.
Visited the company. Learned how work is done in the company and received introduction to internship and project.
Learned how to create database and tables in PostgreSQL and practiced basic SQL queries.
Established connection with PostgreSQL and learned how to display user data from the database.
Learned GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE APIs and built a basic server using Express.js.
Learned basics of Node.js and React.js along with basic TypeScript concepts.
Created estimate timesheet for the project planning.
Created sitemap of the project and reviewed UI references for the Online Job Portal website.
Started working on UI of the project and created pages such as login and registration.
Learned backend handling using Node.js and studied user authentication concepts.
Connected login form with API. On button click, data was inserted into database using React forms and API calls.
Created database and tables in PostgreSQL according to the project sitemap.
Added additional UI pages and finalized UI design for the project.
Developed login and registration functionality in backend using Node.js and Express.js.
Started development of the landing page (Home page) of the website.
Completed design and development of the Home page using React.
page using React.js and Node.js with Express APIs.
page using React.js and Express APIs.
Performed end-to-end module testing for Home, Authorization, and Worker features.
The application includes a secure user authentication system.
Users can register using the Registration form.
Login functionality allows users to access the website using valid credentials.
Input validation ensures correct user data during registration and login.
User data is stored securely in the database.
The application includes worker and client registration with validation for required fields.
The login module authenticates users using email and password and returns a secure JWT token.
The flow includes OTP request and verification support, password change functionality, and protected access to authenticated routes.
Frontend authentication state is stored and reused to maintain session flow across the application.
{ name, email, password, role, phone, address } =
// 'INSERT INTO users (name, email, password, role, phone, address) VALUES ($1,$2,$3,$4,$5,$6) RETURNING
// [name, email, hashed, role, phone || null, address || null]
Insert screenshots of Login, Register, and Auth Flow output here
Insert your screenshot or scanned certificate here before final submission.
The Home module provides the landing experience for both clients and workers.
It includes hero banners, call-to-action buttons, featured categories, navigation links, testimonials, and footer sections.
The page was designed to be responsive and easy to navigate on desktop and mobile devices.
The interface highlights the platform's main purpose of hiring staff quickly and enabling workers to find opportunities.
="relative flex h-auto min-h-screen w-full flex-col group/design-root overflow-x-hidden
px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8 max-w-7xl mx-auto py-3">
font-bold leading-tight tracking-[-0.015em]">Quick Staff Hiring Portal
="layout-content-container flex flex-col w-full max-w-7xl flex-1 px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8">
The Fastest Way to Hire On-Demand Staff
Connect with skilled gig workers or find your next job in minutes.
font-bold leading-normal tracking-[0.015em] hover:bg-gray-200 dark:hover:bg-gray-800 transition-
Insert screenshots of the Home page output here
Insert your screenshot or scanned certificate here before final submission.
The Worker module includes dashboard statistics, scheduled jobs, job history, saved clients, and profile management.
Workers can accept, reject, start, complete, and undo job status changes through integrated APIs.
The module supports worker profile editing, availability validation, service details, and profile picture handling.
Dashboard data is fetched dynamically from worker profile and booking records to show practical performance information.
("No authentication token found. Please log in first.");
|| 'Unknown error'}. Please make sure you are logged in and the server is
Insert screenshots of the Worker dashboard, jobs, and profile output here
Insert your screenshot or scanned certificate here before final submission.
Authentication: JWT, Express Validator, React Hook Form, Yup
Tools: VS Code, Postman, Git, GitHub
Understood company workflow, internship expectations, and the project architecture.
Started analysis of authorization flow and user-role requirements.
Reviewed frontend and backend folder structure used in the project.
Worked on login and registration forms with validation and API integration.
Studied secure authentication flow using JWT-based access control.
Improved form handling, route navigation, and error messages.
Developed the Home module with hero section, categories, and informational content blocks.
Improved responsive design and public navigation structure.
Connected Home page actions to registration and login pages.
Worked on Worker dashboard, job list views, and booking-based data display.
Integrated worker status update APIs and tested action flows.
Added user feedback handling for successful and failed operations.
Completed worker-side enhancements including saved clients, profile features, and validation updates.
Performed testing, bug fixing, and final documentation work.
Prepared internship report content based on completed project modules.
Gained hands-on experience in full-stack web development using React.js, Node.js, Express.js, and PostgreSQL.
Learned practical API integration, form validation, role-based access control, and database-backed feature design.
Improved understanding of responsive UI development and modular component structure.
Improved time management, communication, and consistency while working on project tasks.
Understood the importance of documentation, review, and maintaining a clear development workflow.
Experienced how an IT company organizes web development work across planning, coding, testing, and reporting.

Challenge: Integrating frontend authentication forms with backend APIs and handling validation errors correctly. Solution: Used structured validation on both frontend and backend, and tested request-response flows carefully.

Challenge: Managing different worker job states such as pending, accepted, in progress, completed, rejected, and cancelled. Solution: Implemented status-based APIs and organized UI screens around clear filters and actions.

Challenge: Fetching dashboard and worker data dynamically without breaking the user experience. Solution: Used dedicated API helper methods, error handling, and conditional rendering for loading states.
Challenge: Maintaining responsive design and clear navigation across public pages and worker screens. Solution: Used reusable layouts, Tailwind CSS utilities, and iterative UI refinement.
Challenge: Converting real development work into formal documentation for academic submission. Solution: Structured the report module-wise and summarized progress week by week.

The internship at LYNX SOFTECH was a valuable learning experience that helped me understand full-stack web application development in a practical environment. Working on the Quick Staff Hiring Portal allowed me to apply academic knowledge to real development tasks and strengthen my technical foundation.

By contributing to the Home, Authorization, and Worker modules, I gained experience in frontend development, backend API integration, authentication flow, and worker-side feature implementation. I also improved my debugging ability, documentation skills, and understanding of professional software development practices.
Overall, this internship improved both my technical and professional skills and gave me confidence to work on real-world web development projects in the future.
https://react.dev/ - For React.js concepts and official documentation
https://expressjs.com/ - For Express.js backend routing and middleware concepts
